description_long: This object is a double-ended sword with two long, gently curved blades extending from either side of a central handle. The blades are metallic silver with a subtle blue pattern or engraving near the base of each blade, adding a decorative touch. The handle is wrapped in a brown material, likely leather or a similar substance, and is flanked by gold-colored guards at each end for hand protection. The overall design is symmetrical, with both blades mirroring each other in shape and size. The sword appears lightweight and balanced, suitable for swift, two-handed use. Its unique double-bladed form and ornamental details suggest it could be a ceremonial or fantasy weapon.
description: A double-ended, curved sword with silver blades, blue engravings, a brown wrapped handle, and gold guards. The design is symmetrical and ornate.
description_view_0: The double-bladed sword is seen at an angle, showing both curved blades, the brown handle, and blue engravings on each blade.
description_view_1: Viewed from the tip, the sword appears as a thin line, with the handle and guards barely visible.
description_view_2: This view mirrors view 0, displaying the opposite side of the sword with both blades, handle, and blue engravings visible.
description_view_3: From the side, the sword is seen edge-on, emphasizing the thinness of the blades and the symmetry of the design.
attribute_handle_design: straight, brown wrapped, cylindrical, with gold-colored guards
